title: Option for HIPAA controls at the Workspace level
excerpt: We have a situation where a single team in our organization has a business case that security requires the HIPAA and Redact settings be turned on in our Enterprise Monday.com instance. It’s a precautionary measure as tha…

posts.author: Hazelnutsoylatte (Ivy Giessen)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have a situation where a single team in our organization has a business case that security requires the HIPAA and Redact settings be turned on in our Enterprise Monday.com instance. It’s a precautionary measure as that group receives tasks generated from a public facing website for the specific purpose of communicating with the public.
The entire rest of our organizational use cases do not require this level of security and are unable to use the File Preview, Gallery, Broadcast, features and they have to login to see their notifications rather than just reading them in email. For this reason, we’ve got certain business areas looking for another tool for their processes, something we’d like to avoid.
If the advanced security could be applied only to specific Workspaces, we could maintain full functionality for the majority of the business areas, while also maintaining the required level of security for the special cases that require it.
Thank you!

posts.author: spencerash (Spencer Ash)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be ideal to have the HIPAA functionality available at the workspace level as opposed to the instance. Similar to Ivy’s comment, our organization has some teams that require this level of security for PHI/PII and other’s that your standard project management for non-sensitive projects – these teams are currently hindered by the lack of shareability of their boards which is limiting their ability to work with groups.

posts.author: ktraverso (Kellie Traverso)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d love to see this functionality! I work on the Product team for a PBM and, while we work with PHI all of the time, the product management tasks/roadmaps that we’d like to share with guests do not include PHI. It would really improve our client experience if we were able to share our development roadmap with them without having to export into .xls.

posts.author: caz.evans (Caz Evans)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this been fixed yet? We are now struggling this this problem?
